items.title: Bystanders praised for courage to stop attempted murder of woman

children.content: A man who "doggedly" tried to kill a woman in a prolonged attack in suburban Perth caught on CCTV has is been jailed for 16 years.

gallery.altText: Two men struggling on the ground.

thumbnail.altText: Two men struggling on the ground.

items.title: Why this is the most interesting Western Derby in a decade

image.altText: Players reach for ball at Western Derby.

children.content: Fremantle sits atop the mountain, with one hand firmly grasping the AFL's minor premiership. West Coast is finally showing sustained improvement. Both sides have something to gain; one has a lot to lose.

items.title: Members of a 'surreal' rescue off WA's second-highest peak reunite

children.content: Hiker Lucy Fairweather reunites with her "heroes" as the RAC Rescue helicopter celebrates its 11,000th rescue in WA.

gallery.altText: Woman sitting on edge of helicopter door with two men standing to her right

items.title: One of WA's longest bridges to be built after Indigenous dispute resolved

children.content: One of Western Australia's longest bridges will be built to fix a road to nowhere in Perth's east — resolving a four-year long deadlock over fears the bridge would destroy an ancient rock shelter and billabong.

image.altText: Lloyd St roundabout

items.title: Judge rejects Woodside's attempt to uncover stink-bomb protest organisers

children.content: Energy giant Woodside fails in its Supreme Court bid to force climate activists to hand over documents about the planning of a stink-bomb protest which prompted the evacuation of its Perth headquarters.

image.altText: woodside

items.title: Perth housing market 'edging past its peak' as growth weakens

children.content: While Perth still has the strongest annual house price growth in Australia, Domain says values across the city have risen at their lowest rate in more than a year.

articles.title: 'Nervous' investors retreat from housing market as downturn broadens

children.content: Australia's property market has entered a downturn after both house and unit prices fell for the first time in more than three years, bringing an end to the recent housing boom.

image.altText: a skyline with houses

gallery.altText: A drone view of houses in a suburb of Perth.
A generic photo of houses in a suburb of Perth.

thumbnail.altText: A drone view of houses in a suburb of Perth.
A generic photo of houses in a suburb of Perth.

images.altText: King Charles at Commonwealth Games opening ceremony
Team Australia arrives at the 2026 Commonwealth Games
Athletes from Team Australia arrive at the 2026 Commonwealth Games

articles.title: Out of necessity is borne a unique and wholly Scottish opening ceremony

children.content: The 2026 Commonwealth Games are like no other that came before them and the opening ceremony was similarly unique in a way that will make it hard to go back.

image.altText: A silhouetted bag piper at the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games opening ceremony.

articles.title: Glasgow was asleep and the Games weren't open as Australia got its first win

children.content: Australia's first victory of the Commonwealth Games comes in a surprise extra session as the bowlers start before the opening ceremony.

image.altText: Kelsey Cottrell high fives Australian women's pairs bowls teammate Dawn Hayman at the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games.

articles.title: Your guide to every day of the Comm Games in Australian time

children.content: With 10 sports crammed into 10 full days of action in Glasgow, it may be hard to keep track of what's on and when at the Commonwealth Games. So, here's the full schedule of every day of the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games with all times and days in AEST.

image.altText: A basketballer spins a ball on his finger, a boxer poses, a weightlifter holds up a barbell, and a wheelchair basketballer.

children.content: The Commonwealth Games are officially opened by King Charles III as many of the 3,000 athletes parade in front of 14,000 spectators. Follow live.

items.title: Commonwealth Games open with tartan, a unicorn and the King in a TARDIS

updates.title: The opening ceremony is over. The 2026 Commonwealth Games have begun!
King Charles officially declares the Games open!

items.title: Automakers in shock as China takes over

children.content: In June, a bulk carrier made an unprecedented delivery of EVs to Australia, sounding an ominous warning to the world's automakers.

image.altText: A large red and white ship sits in a dock as a white car is driven off its ramp

items.title: Security concerns in US over Chinese ships that visit Australia

children.content: Two Chinese polar research ships that make regular port visits in Australia have come under increased scrutiny by US authorities targeting "any detected malign activity".

image.altText: Two red and white ships break through an icy body of water.

items.title: Rusted tin transformed into prize-winning portraits

children.content: Pilbara artist Jenna Pickering transforms found bush objects into hyper-realistic portraits as artist-in-residence at the remote Cossack Art Awards.

items.title: First suspected positive case of H5 bird flu detected in Queensland

children.content: It's the first wild, migratory seabird in Queensland to return a suspect positive case for H5 bird flu.

image.altText: Beach in front Tangalooma Resort on Moreton Island off Brisbane on May 1, 2018.

items.title: Interest rates don't just hit households. Small business is feeling the pinch

children.content: Small-to-medium sized businesses have had a challenging year, with increased costs and regulatory reforms. Insolvency rates are beginning to rise and more owners are shopping around for credit.

image.altText: Worker with mask on welding

items.title: Friday news quiz: World Cup winners, rock legends and Barnaby Joyce's dog

children.content: We have 10 questions to test your knowledge of the week in … SIT DOWN! Sorry, it's the ABC news quiz.

items.title: After Brat, Charli xcx asks a bigger question about legacy

children.content: On her new album Music, Fashion, Film, British artist Charli xcx discusses the enduring legacy of art and artists, and how creative figures are remembered long after their moment has passed.

image.altText: Charli xcx stands on a red carpet in a black dress surrounded by fans holding green records

items.title: Historical journals give man past after he spent years making one up

children.content: For most of his life, Cam Sojan knew nothing of his family history until he stumbled upon a historical society in Western Australia's Midwest last year, which revealed his Sikh ancestry.

items.title: New US tariff on Australia 'makes no sense', defence minister says

children.content: Defence Minister Richard Marles has defended Australia's modern slavery laws after the US said it would slap 12.5 per cent tariffs on Australia. Follow live.

image.altText: Richard Marles wear black suit jacket, white shirt and blue with pink dotted tie.

items.title: ASX set to fall as oil tops $US100

children.content: Australian shares are set to fall as oil prices surge above $US100 a barrel and Wall Street's tech sell-off deepens. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A graphic showing the words ASX and stock.

items.title: Labor warned 'sleeper issue' driving One Nation support

children.content: The ALP's 50th National Conference kicked off with the rise of One Nation, local council budgets and regional voter frustrations leading negotiations.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese smiles while standing at a podium. The words "Delivering Real Change" appear on the podium and on the wall

items.title: 'Why would I do that?': Boy in court charged with murdering mother, stabbing sister

children.content: A 16-year-old boy is charged with murdering his mother and attempting to kill his sister in Perth's south-east, as police say the girl is being treated in hospital for "very serious" stab wounds.

children.content: Richard Carapaz almost hits a fan distracted by a TV camera as the Ecuadorian claims the 18th stage of the Tour de France.

image.altText: A fan gets in the way of cyclists.

items.title: 'Fascinating world': McRae questions booing directed at Quaynor

children.content: Collingwood franks its strong run of form with an impressive 34-point win over Adelaide, as Crows fans defy pleas from the club and boo Isaac Quaynor.

items.title: 'Terribly expensive for no service': Claims new mobile maps still not accurate

children.content: Telcos were forced to publish mobile phone coverage maps to new standards last month, but some Telstra customers say they still do not reflect reality.

image.altText: Man holding a mobile phone to his earn sits in a chair on his front porch with a dog at his feet

items.title: Graphic injury photos shown at Yung Filly rape trial

children.content: A doctor testifies at a sexual assault trial about the injuries allegedly inflicted by an internationally renowned YouTube star on an infatuated fan in his Perth hotel room after a concert.

image.altText: A man with African appearence leaves a court building.

items.title: 'These things didn't happen': Senior Catholic cleric denies sex abuse

children.content: The lawyer for a former Catholic bishop who allegedly sexually abused three young men tells his trial the incidents did not happen, and warns the jury not to be affected by possible prejudice against the church.

articles.title: Former Catholic bishop sexually abused boys at 'shirts-off' parties, court hears

children.content: One of Australia's longest serving senior Catholic clerics had a sexual interest in young Aboriginal men and abused them in Western Australia's far north over several years, a Perth court hears.

image.altText: A close-up shot of the former Bishop of Broome, Christopher Alan Saunders, arriving at court in Perth, in front of another man.

children.content: Police allege the 42-year-old stabbed a woman multiple times with a knife while dealing drugs on Kalgoorlie's main street.

items.title: Minister says Australian IS-linked 'Doctor Jihad' can 'rot' in Iraqi jail

children.content: Perth paediatrician Tareq Kamleh went to Syria to join the Islamic State group in 2015 and has, until now, been presumed dead.
